title: New isolated symplectic singularities with trivial fundamental group
speaker: Daniel Juteau
affiliation: CNRS, Université Paris Diderot; Member, School of Mathematics
abstract: In 2000, Arnaud Beauville introduced the notion of symplectic singularities and raised the question of classifying isolated symplectic singularities with trivial local fundamental group: the latter condition is meant to avoid the numerous quotient singularities, since their classification would become intractable as the dimension increases. Actually, he asked whether there was any example beyond minimal nilpotent orbit closures in simple Lie algebras.
tags: Workshop on Representation Theory and Geometry

proof_stages.label: Beauville problem and local fundamental group
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines symplectic singularities, recalls standard sources and Beauville’s theorem, and sharpens the classification question to isolated symplectic singularities with trivial local fundamental group.

proof_stages.label: Slodowy-slice mechanism for isolated singularities
proof_stages.summary: Nilpotent orbit inclusions are converted into transverse Slodowy slices, with minimal degenerations producing isolated symplectic singularities equipped with centralizer actions and a contracting \(\mathbb C^*\)-action.

proof_stages.label: Classical Kraft-Procesi baseline
proof_stages.summary: The known minimal degenerations in classical nilpotent cones are reviewed, showing via row/column removal that they reduce to ADE surface singularities or minimal nilpotent orbit closures.

proof_stages.label: Exceptional nilpotent degenerations and the \(E_8\) seed
proof_stages.summary: The exceptional-type analysis is described, culminating in the difficult \(E_8\) codimension-four degeneration whose full slice is identified, using the \(SL_5\) centralizer and a dimension argument, with a cover of the \(\mathfrak{sl}_5\) nilpotent cone.

proof_stages.label: Dihedral Calogero-Moser family and contracting equations
proof_stages.summary: The \(d=5\) seed is generalized through dihedral symplectic quotients and rational Cherednik/Calogero-Moser spaces, where explicit equations and a coordinate change produce a contracting isolated symplectic family.

proof_stages.label: Triviality of the local fundamental group
proof_stages.summary: Using the contracting action, the local fundamental group is computed from the complement of the singular point via a determinant fibration whose generators become trivial on simply connected surfaces.

proof_stages.label: Newness and equivalence of the constructions
proof_stages.summary: The singularities are shown to be new for \(d\ge 5\) using the Poisson-induced Lie algebra on the cotangent space, and the nilpotent-cover, blow-up, Calogero-Moser, and Nakajima-quiver descriptions are identified as the same analytic singularity.

proof_stages.label: Further descriptions, extensions, and comparison invariants
proof_stages.summary: Final remarks give orbit-closure descriptions, speculate about unequal parameters and higher complex reflection groups, and discuss comparison tools such as torsion in intersection cohomology, Hilbert series, and magnetic quivers.
